interfaces.name: TableProcessor
interfaces.signalFlowRole: Lookup table step in signal path - maps CC input through a user-defined curve when UseTable is enabled

parameterGroups.label: Controller Selection
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters define which MIDI source is read and what value is used before any message arrives

parameters.name: ControllerNumber
parameters.description: The MIDI controller number to respond to (1-127 for CC, 128 for aftertouch, 129 for pitch wheel)

parameters.name: DefaultValue
parameters.description: The initial value before any MIDI CC message is received

parameterGroups.label: Response
parameterGroups.rationale: These parameters shape how the raw MIDI value is transformed: inversion flips the direction, and the table provides a custom mapping curve

parameters.name: UseTable
parameters.description: Enables a lookup table for custom MIDI CC response curves

parameterGroups.label: Smoothing
parameterGroups.rationale: Controls temporal smoothing to reduce zipper noise from discrete 7-bit MIDI CC values

parameters.name: SmoothTime
parameters.description: Smoothing time in milliseconds to reduce zipper noise on value changes

gaps.question: What is the exact processing order? Specifically: MIDI CC -> normalize (0-127 to 0-1) -> invert -> table lookup -> smooth, or a different order?
gaps.context: The pseudo-code must show the correct transformation chain from raw CC value to final modulation value

gaps.question: How do ControllerNumber 128 (aftertouch) and 129 (pitch wheel) work? For aftertouch, is it channel or polyphonic? For pitch wheel, how is the 14-bit value normalized compared to the dedicated PitchWheel modulator?
gaps.context: These special modes change the MIDI source entirely and users need to understand the differences vs. dedicated modulators

gaps.question: How are 7-bit CC values (0-127) normalized to 0-1? Simple division by 127, or some other mapping?
gaps.context: Determines whether CC 64 maps to exactly 0.5 or slightly off

gaps.question: Where does the TableProcessor lookup occur in the signal path? Does it receive the normalized (and possibly inverted) value?
gaps.context: The table input domain determines what the X axis represents to the user

gaps.question: Is DefaultValue used only at initialization, or does it reset when all-notes-off is received? Does it go through the same inversion/table/smoothing chain?
gaps.context: Users need to know when and how the default value applies, especially for instruments that need a specific starting state

gaps.question: What smoothing algorithm is used (exponential, linear ramp, low-pass filter)? Is it the same implementation as PitchWheel's SmoothTime?
gaps.context: The smoothing type affects the feel of controller response

notes: Very similar structure to PitchWheel but with additional ControllerNumber selector and DefaultValue. The special controller numbers 128/129 effectively turn this into an aftertouch or pitch wheel modulator, creating overlap with dedicated modules. The ControllerNumber range starts at 0, not 1 as the user-facing description suggests - CC 0 (Bank Select) is valid. Natural seeAlso candidates: PitchWheel, Aftertouch, Velocity.
